---
title: My dynamic report
output: html_fragment
---

```{r setup, include=FALSE}
knitr::opts_chunk$set(echo=FALSE, message=FALSE, warning=FALSE, error=FALSE)

```

```{css}
body { 
      font-family: Arial, sans-serif;
      background-color: #d0e4f7
      }
h2 { color: #2E86AB; }
```

## Library Activities: Observations of Activity Types and Group Sizes

The purpose of this dynamic report is to analyse observations collected in the library, such as what activities people are doing, whether they are alone or in a group, and the size of the groups. 

```{r}
#Load packages
library(tidyverse)

#Load Data
logged_data <- read_csv("https://docs.google.com/spreadsheets/d/e/2PACX-1vT6WW3S0g1VRTCFdQ40KSfTgRUKG78DaOyIpo10sTyd4cyTtRqULdiaymaFq4Azv7dUV4prBdzq-Ebt/pub?output=csv")

#Renaming variables
latest_data <- logged_data %>%
  rename(
    Activity = `What activity is the person doing?`,
    AloneGroup = `Is the person alone or in a group?`,
    GroupSize = `How many people are in the group?`
  )
```

```{r}
#Bar chart of activities 
latest_data %>%
  ggplot() +
  geom_bar(aes(x = Activity)) +
  labs(
    title = "Distribution of Library Activities",
    x = "Activity",
    y = "Count"
  ) +
  theme(plot.background = element_rect(fill = "#d0e4f7", color = NA),
    panel.background = element_rect(fill = "#d0e4f7", color = NA)
  )
```

```{r}
activity_counts <- latest_data %>%
  count(Activity)

paste0(
  "The most common activity is ", 
  activity_counts$Activity[which.max(activity_counts$n)],
  " with ", max(activity_counts$n), " observations, and the least common activity is ",
  activity_counts$Activity[which.min(activity_counts$n)], 
  " with ", min(activity_counts$n), " observations."
)
```
